mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents through standardized communication protocols. It simplifies the deployment complexity and enhances security for agent-based applications, making it ideal for production environments. Key trade-offs include the need for a Kubernetes cluster and the requirement for specific configurations for optimal performance.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is necessary for managing the Kagenti deployment.

```bash

curl -Lo kagenti-cli https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli && sudo mv kagenti-cli /usr/local/bin/

```

> This command downloads the CLI, makes it executable, and moves it to a directory in your PATH.
commands: curl -Lo kagenti-cli https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli && sudo mv kagenti-cli /usr/local/bin/

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in your chosen region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to connect to the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig with the cluster's credentials.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Install Kagenti
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti application on your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti && helm repo update

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti --create-namespace

```

> Adding the Kagenti Helm repository.
> This installs Kagenti in its own namespace.
commands: helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti && helm repo update

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/autoscale.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This applies the autoscaling configuration.
> This applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti application and its associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> This command uninstalls the Kagenti release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your configurations and data. This step includes draining nodes if necessary, performing the upgrade, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl get pods --namespace kagenti

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti

kubectl get pods --namespace kagenti

```

> Check the status of the pods before proceeding.
> This command upgrades Kagenti to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubernetes cluster unreachable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig may not be set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1' to refresh your kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install chart: ...'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm chart version may not be compatible with your Kubernetes version.

**Fix:**
Ensure you are using a compatible version of the Helm chart with your Kubernetes version.

**Affected versions:** <= 1.30

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be an issue with the application configuration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace kagenti' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the Kagenti deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new agent communication protocols and improved security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods --namespace kagenti' showing all pods in 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: curl -Lo kagenti-cli https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli && sudo mv kagenti-cli /usr/local/bin/
helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti && helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
